name: Abrahamson, Lida
motto: "Gentle in manner, vigorous in performance."
description: Here's Lida, always merry and always in a hurry. If diligent work and perseverance bring success Lida will attain it. She holds the presidency of one organization and has a natural monopoly on the office of vice-president. Her great motto: "There's no time like the present."

name: Baldwin, John
motto: "My own thoughts are my companions."
description: And Baldwin always travels in good company. A thinker who expounds on any given thought willingly. An Arts grad. who decided to get in with a bunch of good fellows, and so took Mining. Has a perfectly lovely time at all the receptions—no higher appreciation can possibly be written.

name: Barnes, Paul
high school: Glen Ullin, N. Dak. Model High
motto: "The great silent man! looking round on the noisy insanity of the world."
description: A sober minded philosopher who watches the parade with a quiet, condescending smile. His "boot" has often saved the "Varsity" from defeat. In some strange way he has secured a mortgage on the fireplace in Davis Hall parlor which thus far no one has been able to wrest from him.

name: Bennington, Norman Wesley
motto: "Let's have a good time, fellows; we'll soon be gone."
description: Why, Wesley? We altogether fail to understand. Benny's career is enough to make his famous namesake turn in his grave and renounce the great Methodist church. An engineer of much skill and renown—perhaps notoriety is the better word. Nevertheless, it is predicted that he will make as big a noise in the professional world as he does at present on the campus.

name: Boise, Howard
motto: "He is not short, he is not tall, He is the lad that courts them all."
description: Howard is noted for his love of a certain flower of the family Orchidaceae and his affinity for fair-haired maidens. His popularity with the fair sex is evinced by the amount of Hershey he can sell in a given time.

name: Bradshaw, Frederick H. Jr.
motto: "Strike one, Strike two, Out on second."
description: A talkative, jolly, companionable fellow. His captivating smile and pleasing manner make him popular with the ladies. An engineer, but not spoiled thereby. When in a particularly pleasant mood, will sing for you. A baseball player of much ability. It is also said that he has made a home run in the more important game where Cupid acts as umpire.

name: Brennan, James J.
motto: "Friend, I'm not looking for flattery, Still, don't you think I am Class?"
description: IT—the other twenty-four letters of the alphabet spell nothing. An able man—if success were measured by stature we tremble to think what he would not do were he six-three. There is a rumor current that Jimmie was unsophisticated during his Freshman year, but up to the present writing it has been impossible to substantiate the report.

name: Brockhoff, Frederick J.
motto: "What should a man do but be merry?"
description: Two synonymous terms: "Brockhoff" and "earnest student." We await with eager interest his little monograph, "The Conservation of Energy," or "How to Graduate Without Work." We predict an unrivalled success—the public today demands assertion from authority. Brock takes an active interest in politics—it is said that the aid he brought the Democratic party was the primal cause of Burtness' success.

name: Bruyere, Alan
motto: "Nature was to him so lavish of her store That she bestowed until she had no more."
description: An authority in Metallurgy. On his frank countenance you see clearly sincerity, honor, ability, good fellowship—and there's nothing wrong with the photograph. Brue recognizes the wisdom of Horace Greeley when he said "Go west, young man, go west," but being conservative he only goes about an eighth of a mile.

name: Budge, Guy C.
motto: "It's a great plague to be too handsome a man."
description: It is a hardship for some people to be handsome, but we must confess that Guy carries it off with the best grace in the world. An incident of the Junior Prom '10 might be mentioned here, but the subject is too exhausting to be pursued. As for other qualities of the mind and heart, some of them are quite unmentionable, and the rest you can guess.

name: Budge, William Edward
motto: "The silence that is golden Makes a halo 'round his head."
description: Bill is a taciturn man and a hard worker; well liked by all and very popular with the professors. He never asks for anything he doesn't want, and therefore is certain to get what he asks for. One can see his light burning all night in the Mines Building as evidence of his industry.

name: Burns, Myrtle Esther Lois
high school: Hannah, N. Dak. Central High, Minneapolis; Hamline U.
motto: "For if she will, she will; you may depend on't, And if she won't, she won't; so there's an end on't."
description: A holder of decided opinions which she defends sturdily. Gifted with a desire for information, which she usually gratifies. Gibraltar might be hard to move but it's not as set in its manner of belief as Myrtle is. Competent, true blue. "Why, when, where, how, —?"

name: Carkin, Theone
motto: "She doeth little kindnesses that most of us leave undone."
description: Theone, better known as "Tony," who had completed her Normal course, heard of the wonderful class of '11, so returned to get the most she could out of two years with us. She has shown to us all that unselfishness is one of the greatest virtues to be acquired. A girl worth while—she was never known to lose a friend.

name: Challoner, George T.
motto: "Some good I mean to do Despite of my own nature."
description: This is one of the mighty engineers who were here before they had an engineering course. Snits has gone away and in his solitude there is nothing George so much resembles as the exhibited portraits of Pharaoh III, second son of Rameses. Unto himself he has collected an array of photographs well worthy the attention of any appreciative man.

name: Clouston, Caroline J.
high school: Pelican Rapids, St. Cloud Normal School
motto: "Falseness cannot come from her."
description: A true hearted woman in whom one can place much trust. She has never been known to cut class or to bluff—it wasn't necessary. An astronomy student who can "bring down" the sun as well as any doughty mariner can.

name: Ellison, Edward O.
motto: "And as for music, he converseth very pleasantly upon the cornet."
description: Ed is uniformly pleasant with everyone, and it is not to be wondered at that he has many friends. The only thing a maiden must look out for is that he really doesn't mean all the nice things he looks. He just simply can't help looking nice. And he is exceedingly careful of what he says as they can ascertain by trying to commit him.

name: Fisher, Lawrence Frederick
motto: "A stoop o' wine, Maria! A stoop o' wine!"
description: Perhaps there are handsomer people than Sir Toby, and some of higher moral standing; but for a certain Epicurean cheerfulness that is at home in any situation, a carelessness of consequences, and a constant, all-prevailing, confident smile—there are none like him.

name: Flett, Charles Magoris
motto: "Methinks he looks as though he were in love."
description: Our star tackle. Magoris at one time felt a great longing for the foaming sea and journeyed to Annapolis. Since then he has eliminated the sea, but his liking for "foam" remains as strong as before. In love with many, but thinks that Devils Lake has the fairest "Garden of Roses."

name: Gilbertson, Carl
motto: "The kings of modern thought are dumb."
description: We think the above should hold Carl for a short time. He has the idea that he is the only living authority on Education with the exception of Schmidtie, but one reason why no one ever disputes his assumption is that he makes it in so apologetic a manner no one dreams he is taking himself seriously. But perhaps he is! He has good reason.

name: Gilroy, Wm. T.
motto: "'Tis deeds must win the prize."
description: And if deeds can do it, Bill is very likely to get there. He is one of the practical electrical engineers, and about the only one who can talk to Dr. Taylor and know absolutely whereof he speaks. He is a heavy-weight in the subjects of switch-boards, ohms, resistances and cross-sections. There may be others.

name: Greenleaf, Wm. H.
motto: "Look at him with awe; consider him with reverence; treat him with the profound respect that is his due."
clubs: State Delegate N. D. O. A.
description: And after going through with these preliminaries you may go up close and talk to him without any fear whatever, for if there is a man at the "U" who carries honors lightly and with ease, it is that same William.

name: Gustafson, T. A.
high school: Reeder, N. D.; Windom, Minn., High; Minnesota University.
motto: "Behavior! What wert thou till this man taught thee? And what art thou now?"
description: Did you ever see him do anything improper? No. Do you ever expect to? No. Do you think anyone will ever find it possible to take exception to his attitude on any occasion? No. Then he's a proper man, isn't he? You bet your life! There's nothing improper about him. The only man in school who didn't read the improper number of Life.

name: Halvorson, Orpheus H.
motto: "O, a most dainty man!"
description: "Ain't he just too cute?" the girls all say, and really he is an amusing, roly-poly little man. His cherubic countenance is usually o'erspread with a smile of pleasure, jollity and mirth—that doesn't leave room for much else. Recites with one of those confidential tones as though conversant with the whole subject.

name: Harvey, Leilla
motto: "Man is a giddy thing, and this is my conclusion."
description: She hath a most radiant smile—and the blackest eyes! Furthermore we have been unable to learn. In classes she is by turns an effective bluffer, a good student, a fiasco, and a lady. At times she is all these together, and at such moments presents an insoluble problem to the student of human psychology.

name: Haskett, Margaret C.
motto: "It's great to be Irish."
description: But very few of us can be so Irish as Margaret and yet be cosmopolitan. The people who do not like her simply do not know her. Beside being a bright and shining star in classes, and known to all of us through the medium of the pen, she is possessed of a ready and kindly wit that makes her the best company in the world.

name: Healey, Ralph L.
high school: Red Lake Falls, Minn., High; Minnesota U.
motto: "He was a man versed in the world, As a pilot in his compass."
description: A complacent, self-sufficient man who never becomes excited. A musician most popular at the Men's Union, where he is the central figure of many a song-fest.

name: Helmer, Myrtle M.
motto: "A brown-eyed maid with a roguish smile."
description: Dainty, debonair, delightful. An English shark of the first degree; ambitious in classes, She is to be found at all social functions—where she is almost too popular to be an entire success with her University sisters. Meanwhile she is doing her share in brightening this dull, dreary world.

name: Holt, Mabel
high school: Blabon, N. Dak. Northwood High; Model High.
motto: "It's all right to love humanity, But I was born a specialist."
description: For particulars go to the little parlor of Davis 'most any time. This vivacious young lady is, in chemical phrase, of slightly acid reaction, and commendable as an active principle wherever found. It is rumored that since she donned her Senior cap she has decided to eschew all slamming. Mabel is one of the most conscientious students of her class.

name: Hunt, Chas. E.
motto: "Ful wel he sange the service devine."
description: Every Sunday morning Charley is matched against the pipe organ of the Methodist church;—thus far the struggle has been almost a draw—the organ having the advantage of age and experience, whilst Charles has the wider range and greater staying power. His Methodist tendencies manifest themselves in other ways—but why tell what you all know?

name: Hydle, Lars L.
motto: "Yet, if men moved him, was he such a storm As oft 'twixt May and April is to see."
description: This sturdy son of Norway came to us from Grand Forks College, where his native loyalty brought him much eminence. He has been prominent here as a man of marked ability, as a student of indefatigable energy, and of strong if rather weird opinions on about everything in general, from marriage to Senior Proms. All joking aside, Hydle is a man who always stands for the right as he sees it, and most particularly for the spirit of real democracy.

name: Joseph, A. Ingram
motto: "For he could distinguish and divide A hair, 'twixt south and southeast side."
description: That only goes to show how precise Joe is in his work. His public appearances are usually only when he punishes the piano. For the greater part he sits on the shore and watches the fleet go by, but on occasion goes forth to do mighty deeds. Would there were more like him!

name: Johnson, Gilbert I.
motto: "He's a man worthy any woman."
description: Almost any afternoon he can be found at the library—where he goes to study Pol. Econ. 'Tis strange that one so democratic as he can be so exclusive at times. To be three years in love exceedeth a college education—he's eligible for a doctor's degree.

name: Knapp, Edgar B.
motto: "Therefore Achilles will never catch up with the tortoise."
description: Burt has been pursuing his University studies for four years now, and though he is evidencing great endurance at the present time, and making manful efforts, the aforesaid subjects are in a fair way to make their escape entirely. Never mind—the brightest man is he who does not let the course interfere with his education.

name: Langer, Frank
motto: "Discretion in speech is more than eloquence."
description: Mr. Langer is one of the busiest men in school, for besides being president of his class he is prominent in half a dozen other activities. As usual with busy people, however, he is always to be relied upon. We need never worry about his future, for if no cases come to him he'll invent some.

name: Leine, Anna
motto: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild."
description: This stately Norse maiden is a prospective teacher and if her looks were an index to her wisdom, she would go well recommended. There is, however, the added fact that she is one of our best students.

name: Lewis, Jennie Belle
motto: "It is foul weather in us all When you are cloudy."
description: Jennie Belle is not exactly what Dr. Stearns would call a "Cyclopedic Sphinx of A's and B's," but she gets them just the same. Even so, her studies never interfere with her college life, and if there is a good time going, Jennie Belle is right after it—and it will be "going some" if it keeps ahead of her. We all know her through her music; some delight in a better acquaintance.

name: Lund, Irwin
motto: "Laughed at all his jokes, For many a joke had he."
description: The perpetrator of many a bum witticism. An authority on the morning after the night before. Irwin came here years ago when the school was small, and notwithstanding its marvelous progress and development, the institution has been unable to keep pace with him. A miner excelled only by Wallace.

name: Martineau, Joseph L.
high school: St. John, N. Dak. Model High
motto: "Why, man, there be good fellows in the world."
description: A jolly good fellow who succeeds in everything he chooses to attempt. In track he is there seven days of the week, and as a soloist he again qualifies—in height, distance and endurance. A worthy member of the best bunch in the institution—the senior medics.

name: Pearl, Monroe
motto: "Good fellowship is the ship for me."
description: Pearl came from Langdon some four years ago to show the University people what a real basketball player looked like. Her specialties are breaking hearts and making baskets, and both as a "breaker" and a "maker" she is certainly a winner.

name: Movious, Herbert J.
motto: "Our partings, though late, appear always too soon."
description: There is not a more consistent man on the campus or off it as far as any one knows. We are not saying in which line he is most thoroughly so, however. Another track man with a double record. The faculty seriously consider withholding his diploma—not because of his grades, but to give the world another year in which to prepare itself against the time of his entering the profession.

name: Nash, Emily B.
motto: "Music, when soft voices die, Vibrates in the memory."
description: Emily came to the University from that source of all good—the Grand Forks High School. She arrived here a year ahead of the rest of us—but it took her a couple of years to decide between being a second Paderewski and graduating with the class of 1911. Like a sensible person she decided on the latter course, for which we are all glad.

name: Eilert, Nesdahl
motto: "Singular in every particular."
description: Which, being translated, means that he countenances no form of double-dealing. If this explanation suits you not, form one of your own. The holder of the Varsity record for asking wooden questions, though there are many competitors. Through this self-same system he has acquired much information—the greater part of it being gratuitous.

name: Neilson, Hazel B.
motto: "Rare compound of jollity, frolic and fun, To relish a joke and rejoice at a pun."
description: It is seldom that one meets a happier, more songful lassie than this sprig of the thistle. But though she admits compatriotism with Bruce and the rest of them, there could be nothing more apt than the way she does the Irish maid on the stage. She is one of the most kindly and pleasant of those who leave us this year.

name: Nestos, Peter
motto: "I've lived and loved."
description: A handsome fellow, who couldn't help fussing if he tried; most popular with the ladies. For complete details see the description of the hero in one of George Barr McCutcheon's novels. All in all, it is no wonder that a hop without his presence is a dismal failure.

name: Olson, Mabel
motto: "In the science lecture-room Knowledge doth she daily drink."
description: So much for the botany shark. It is not within the range of human endeavor to lead this lady to the point of looking into your eyes or doing anything else which is notably frivolous. She has cares above the mundane and the romantic. Perhaps we all will some time. Who knows?

name: Oftedal, Axel
motto: "The heart of honor, the tongue of truth."
description: "Doc" learned the art of being a gentleman while teaching in a high school at an outpost of civilization. He is a finished courtier—having profited by the course he took at Mayville Normal. Moreover, there are many here who are willing to swear by the quality of steel in this chased scabbard.

name: Poupore, Ethel M.
high school: Grand Forks High. St. Catherine's Academy.
motto: "To those that know thee not, no words can paint, And those who know thee, know all words are faint."
description: Ethel entered our class in her Sophomore year, but since then she has made up for the time lost by not being a Freshman with us. When you want a thing well done, Ethel is there every time. This may account for the number of baskets she shot, as well as the number of "A's" found in her credit book.

name: Pullin, George
motto: "Nature hath framed strange fellows in her time."
description: A little of the unusual is good for us—a break in the monotony is always welcomed. A man of extraordinary ability—the doer of many deeds recorded in history. The knight of many a "faire ladye." Below we give a brief summary of his victories in the tournament of Love:

name: Rex, Jeannette M.
motto: "Her very frowns are fairer far Than smiles of other maidens are."
description: So quiet and reserved is she that it is hard to say whether we know her or not. But she is not of the kind that is unkind, and her smile—if you are fortunate—and why analyze our pleasures! Let us enjoy them and be content.

name: Rightly, Thomas J.
high school: St. John's Military Academy, Colorado School of Mines, Kansas University
motto: "He is so young: hardly of age, or little more than that."
description: A most effervescent man who wastes more enthusiasm than most of us can collect in a year. One thinks of him in the same breath as a miniature whirlwind, or the simoon. He holds many original ideas as to the engineer's future place in the world—you'll notice we didn't say "future world."

name: Robertson, Clarence W.
motto: "I will be somebody yet."
description: Give Boss a vaulting pole and he will rise very high in the world. Classes do not bother him; he never lets thoughts of study interfere with existence. An authority on many diversified subjects—which knowledge he has derived from much original investigation. When asked his opinion of the girls, he expresses it tersely and thusly: "Divine!"

name: Robertson, Clarence L.
motto: "Heart whole and fancy free."
description: If the volume of a man's laugh is the measure of his mirth, then Robertson must be the happiest man in school; for day and night his hearty laugh may be heard echoing down the corridors of Budge. He is one of the mighty ones in Teachers College; we prophesy a grand time for the fortunate high school pupils who will next year address him as "Professor."

name: Ruby, Wesley
motto: "For he by geometric scale Could take the size of pots of ale."
description: It would be hard to say why the suggestion of his pronomen has so little effect upon so eminently unclerical a character. Suffice it to say that he is distinguished more for conscientiousness than church-going, and that he gives each professor the impression of being a "find."

name: Russell, Ben
motto: “Rapt in the solitude of his own individuality.”
description: He’s a shark, all right, and there’s no getting around it. For that matter, we don’t want to get around it, because we know a good fellow when we see him. He’s neither egotistical nor selfish, and his admirers are legion because those who chat once with him become his friends.

name: Schmidt, Edward J.
motto: “What's the use of hurrying, fellows; there's plenty of time.”
description: Having the potentialities of a true Will Honeycomb, Ed has kept the fellows in suspense and the girls in a state of nervous inquietude for a weary three years—and to no avail. Noted for his repartee in Spanish class. A hard worker—but also recognizes the advantages of bluffing.

name: Slingsby, Emma
motto: “Gentle of speech, beneficent of mind.”
description: Her delight is in athletics; she is a member of the famous '11 basketball team. As a result of her stay she has a well filled credit book and a host of friends among the girls. Her quiet, reserved manner prevents many from knowing her, but she has all the more to give to those whom she chooses to know.

name: Smith, Norman L.
motto: “So doth the little busy bee.”
description: One thing about Norman is certain. You can’t talk to him five minutes without finding it out, and he’ll never let you forget it. That is that he is specializing in economics. For him that is the only subject under the sun; all other learning is extraneous to the real needs of man. We won’t contradict him. We don’t like to argue.

name: Steele, Clarence O.
motto: “I know the gentleman to be of worth, and worthy estimation.”
description: A man who combines the practical and the cultural educations most satisfactorily. More unusual than that, he also unites oratory and physical training—his speeches are replete with many gestures. Much interested in astronomy—has seen the moon in more phases from the coulee bridge than any one else here.

name: Steele, Reuben
motto: “A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays and confident tomorrows.”
description: A primitive, primeval, primordial man of the stamp which marks the Vikings. Our “bestest” guard. Can always be found talking to at least seven girls, but on occasion has shown partiality. Nothing disturbs him; he forges steadily ahead at his own sweet will.

name: Sutherland, R. Leslie
motto: "How many fine people there are in this world if you'll only scratch them deep enough."
description: A quiet and industrious man who says little. During his stay here he has been little known outside his class—he was too busy making a name for himself. Never mixed—but he has the respect and admiration of those who know him. 'Tis rumored that after awhile he's going to cut his biplane loose.

name: Sutton, Lois Madeline
high school: Prior Lake, Minn.; U. of Minnesota; U. of Chicago.
motto: "Who can say more than this praise, That you alone are you?"
description: If one should happen to discover the fair Madeline sitting upon the parapet of a Spanish castle with her robes of satin flowing about her and her hair bound with the customary rose ribbon, one would not be in the least surprised. She looks like the heroine of one of the old romances. But she really believes in woman's suffrage, and is very sensible.

name: Templeton, Maude R.
motto: "For she was 'jes' the quiet kind Whose natures never vary, Like streams that keep a summer mind Snow-hid in January."
clubs: Executive Board of the Women's League
description: Maude's strong point is picking the stiffest course in the catalogue. Geology and Math. are here included; she took them not because there were boys in the class,—in spite of, rather than because of them. She is a firm believer in a liberal education, her specialties at present being astronomy and cooking. But then—both may be useful, in the order named, in the near future.

name: Terrett, Fannie Rosalie
motto: "If you should wish I were exact, Why, she's bewitching—that's a fact."
description: When a "Freshie," Fannie won the hearts of all when she appeared as a French doll. She has entered into almost every activity of University life, getting as much fun out of it as could be found. The girls of Larimore have crowned her "Queen of the Chafing Dish."

name: Tombs, Hazel
motto: "She knows how to live and keep the middle path."
description: Hazel has already engaged to join the ranks of those who wield the birch, but we predict that her sway will be as gentle and unassuming as her manner. She never suffers her spirits to be influenced by her cognomen.

name: Tufft, Harry E.
motto: "Look you, I am most concerned with my own interests."
description: But in spite of the fact that he minds his own affairs entirely, Tufft is a very companionable fellow. It's the Irish of him—his fund of amusing experiences is apparently inexhaustible. But one should have tales to tell when they have engaged in as many industries as he has.

name: Tweto, Alma
motto: "I think no virtues go with sizes."
description: Alma has played her role well in every activity which she has entered. As a member of debating teams she has shown her dramatic ability. Alma has always been a "booster" for 1911, and in her Junior year was an excellent president. Though a great student of history, nevertheless she thinks of a few dates of the present, and every week looks for the "Buster" page in the funny supplement.

name: Veitch, Bernice
motto: "Her beauty is exquisite, but her favor infinite."
description: Not to know her is to argue oneself a Freshman and an unaspiring one. She has the optimism of the Sphinx with none of that worthy lady's immobility, for Bernice is a merry, merry maid. We have been cheated in not having kept her more than two years.

name: Woods, Donald K.
motto: "Fie, what a spendthrift he is of his tongue."
description: Early in his precocious career Don mastered a large vocabulary with which to enlighten the world with his erudite and perpetual conversation. When matched against a phonograph he easily demonstrates the superiority of mind over matter. A jovial optimist; game to the end.

name: Christie, Howard C.
motto: "A harmless flaming meteor shone for hair, And fell about his ears with careless air."
description: 'Twas cruel of him not to have his photo taken. As first baseman for the medics he presents a variegated spectacle. A studious medic—persevering and diligent. His walk copyrighted. We were going to bawl him out in the Dacotah, but the faculty said it wasn't necessary.

name: Donovan, Benjamin F.
motto: "A prodigy of learning, a rhapsody of words."
description: The only theologian in the class. His is a wonderful vocabulary—all the more noteworthy since, because of his profession, it is perforce expurgated. A diligent bearer of the Light—he fights the good fight.

name: Anderson, James Maxwell
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous."
description: Whatever there may be to say for him, certainly there is nothing to be said against his record in classes. He is a general favorite with himself, and was never known to lose confidence in the world's appreciation. At times he writes poetic prose; when inspired he even writes poetic poetry. The philosophers are his favorites—he is our greatest exponent of the intellectual bluff.

name: Bates, Marie Alice
motto: "A maid of grace, and complete majesty."
description: Marie is our latest acquisition, just secured from the wilds of Leland Stanford. She is a student of the super-excellent kind—delights in Greek masterpieces; is an authority on the classicists—whose disciple she is and a possible rival. Fortunately her studies never interfere with her good time and she keeps her sense of humor intact. Why write?—she's here—lucky U. N. D.!

name: Batten, Annabel Hawley
motto: "I will have it so because I will have it so."
description: Our Madam Pankhurst. Should Annabel lend her logic and her voice to the cause of forensic oratory, our young men would perchance look long for their laurels. But she chooses not Portia's calling, rather will her gifts be given to the more profitable cause of suffrage. The indomitable defender of women's rights; a believer in the brotherhood of man.

name: Blewett, Joseph A.
high school: St. John's Academy, St. Thomas College
motto: "A glass is good and a lass is good, And a pipe to smoke in cold weather; The world is good and the people are good, And we're all good fellows together."
description: Joe is the original "silicon kid"; he is the toughest man in the class. Though infusible and noncombustible, he smokes much. An original wit who uses his wits in lieu of study. An athletic star of the first magnitude—football, baseball, basketball, highballs, all happen naturally to him. To hear him recite the benefits of studying Geology is illuminating, edifying and satisfying.

name: Bliss, Emma Marie
motto: "Cease not in strong endeavor."
description: Since her arrival from the Normal, Miss Bliss has undertaken the stupendous task of enforcing rules in Macnie—a task worthy of a courage of the most intrepid kind. A good student, ever diligent, she may regard with satisfaction her increasing store of "A's."

name: Brannon, Raymond
motto: "Silent in seven languages."
description: So quiet that you never know he's around until the marks are handed out. This photograph will undoubtedly satisfy much curiosity—many people having seen his name in the Directory naturally wondered who he was. A poor mixer, to those who know him he is the best of good fellows.

name: Campbell, Bessie Margaret
motto:
"A cheerful mien, a happy smile
Is what announces her."
description: Another of the people of Pembina who descended upon the University with the intent of leaving only what they are not able to carry away. Bess has done her share in upholding the reputation of the bunch—behold her array of A's. A good, dutiful lass, who never dreamed of even wondering how it would feel to break rules.

name: Chandler, Newell E.
motto:
"Out upon it! I have loved
Three whole days together."
description: The most accomplished fusser at the U—and the most inconstant. You never know where he'll be next—and he doesn't, either. His spirits rise from absolute zero to 100° C.; it all depends upon the temperature of her smile. Luckily his fussing is of the continual and not the continuous variety—therefore he is left time to be a good student, and the truest kind of a friend.

name: Covert, Beth
motto:
"She grew to womanhood and between whiles
Rejected several suitors, just to learn
How to accept a better in his turn."
description: Beth has taken part in all kinds of sports—from track work to skiing. She believes in variety in all things, classes being also included in this term. Of them all, Beth is the one most in need of a filing system and card index. Why? Just 'cause.

name: Thorvald, Dahl
motto: "I am no orator, as Brutus is, But as you know me all, a plain, silent man."
description: A consistent worker, a mighty good fellow who enjoys life without interfering with anyone else's pleasure. Came to us with a large vocabulary and obstinate opinions—now see what he is! Can never learn to run a bluff, but acquired a ready laugh which he uses when the instructor produces a joke. "Yes, yes, don't you remember? He was seen with a girl once, the first year."

name: Devine, Lois Virginia
high school: Wheeling High, Wiley School for Women
motto: "She who is convinced against her will Is of her own opinion still."
description: Lois Virginia came to far-away North Dakota to become wise to the startling ways of doing things in the West. Whatever she may think of the experiment, 'twas a great day for the U. N. D. In spite of her many threats to "just die off," we hope she will long remain with us. At her Cupid has shot many arrows but apparently only for the pleasure of again straightening them.

motto: "A sunny face and disposition, truly."
description: One of the most loyal and most approachable of the Junior Girls. She says little about her class work, but that is necessary only for those who need the advertising. She has found what the philosophers have long sought—undisturbed happiness. "It's great to have a brother at school—especially some one else's brother."

She has found what the philosophers have long sought—undisturbed happiness. "It's great to have a brother at school—especially some one else's brother."

name: Ebner, George
motto: "I never dare to write As funny as I can."
major: Arts, Civil Eng., A. D. T., Civics Club, Synergoi, Dacotah Board.
description: Our optimistic pessimist—famous more for what he has left undone than what he has accomplished. Our biographer—eclipsed only by Boswell and Plutarch—in spite of the fact that they had poorer subject material. Eb has such an over-developed sense of humor that he can even see the joke when it's on himself. Once considered going out for football, but decided that the work was too hard for the grub. Persevering—or is it stubbornness?

name: Elkin, Clarence L.
motto: "I am the top of the pinnacle, I am the swell little chap."
description: Eckie is the very center of our solar system—the planets may now begin revolving to some purpose. A clever student. The ghosts of Blackstone, Webster and Whitelaw Reid recognize in him their only rival. Whatever his future as a lawyer, he pleads his "first case" every now and then with flattering success.

name: Elznic, William H.
motto: "A very honest-hearted fellow."
description: Together with Adam Smith, Bill is recognized as an authority on all economic matters. He has a collection of "B" marks that he adds to constantly. A man of common sense, at times deigning to unloosen a bit. There's the incident of the Junior Prom, you know when Bill again came up to the mark. When he desires solitude, brings forth his trombone. Bill can tell you the latest song hit at the Grand.

name: Freeman, B. Farrow
motto: "He has a facile mind."
description: In fact, he is able to turn his attention to anything on earth which is supposed to be of a curious nature. His tendency is not really immoral, as some have supposed; he merely wishes to satisfy his curiosity upon every subject under the sun, which worthy object he is rapidly attaining. Fussed once Freshman year, but had such a narrow escape from matrimony that he hasn't dared try since.

name: Donna, Mae Forkner
high school: Wahpeton, N. D. State School of Science
motto: "Happy am I, from care I'm free; Why aren't they all contented like me?"
description: As merry as the day is long—and the days are getting longer all the time. Of much literary ability, she writes those little quips which express much. Her genius lies in her vivacity and kindliness. Enough—of all the people in the world she needs a press agent least.

name: Gullickson, Oscar
motto: "I go, I go; swifter than arrow From Tartar's bow!"
description: An athlete of much renown. As a star he makes more of a glare than our visitor, the comet, ever thought of producing. Added to that he has a batting average of a thousand with his Profs. The small boys of Park River regard him as the town's finest product—and it produces many men of fame. "Aw, Mr. Umpire, I didn't push him, honestly!"

name: Griffin, Bernice Marie
motto: "Her genius is her friendliness."

A Geology shark of the most notable kind; she has a sort of affinity for the hard courses given here. She states that she has not yet decided upon her life work—this will be welcome news to many.

name: Raymond, A. Heising
motto: "The mind's the measure of the man."
description: And at that rate Heising is 99 44/100 pure. The only possible criticism is that he minds his own business—a strange occurrence in this day and age. A Math. shark. A most efficient engineer. It is to be hoped that for his thesis he will solve the enigma of the age—that which baffles the keenest minds of the time: Explain from the point of view of shear, stress, strain, torque, and bending moment the ability of the "columnar neck of alabaster" to uphold the present-day load of hat and hair.

name: Richard, Leo Hennessey
motto: "And then he will talk,—great Gods, how he will talk!"
description: Our theologian, politician, and ladies' man—a success in the order named. He talks at any time, on any subject, easily, fluently, vivaciously, inevitably, for personal pleasure. A maker of history—fortunately of the kind that doesn't repeat itself. "Say, professor, what grade did I make today? I am working hard, enjoying the lessons, and expect to make an A for the semester."

name: Blondie, A. Holt
motto: "And she is fair, and fairer than that word."
description: Our president is the favorite of the class but is in no degree affected thereby. Loyal, a good student and of the kindest disposition in the world. But why write a "writeup"?—if you like her, you don't want it; if you don't, you don't deserve it. "Is Blondie fair because she's named Blondie, Or named 'Blondie' because she's fair?"

name: Hodgins, Stella E.
motto: "Virtues hath she many mo' Than I with pen have skill to show."
description: A holder of decided opinions which she champions stoutly. A friend of all the girls; of the fellows, one. Stella is one of our most popular girls. Cheerful and gay, always ready to do her part, she has won a place in the hearts of all. A well known basketball player, but she excels in track work.

name: Howland, Garth A.
motto: "Make not thyself the slave of any woman."
description: It was prophesied that by spring Garth would have discovered that there were girls on the campus. However, he still burns midnight oil in Hull House and goes to class with a precision not usually ascribed to an Arts student of the masculine gender. If he were less of a cynic he would be a better Methodist.

name: Hyslop, Clara Ross
motto: "Nor bold nor shy, nor short nor tall, But a new mingling of them all."
description: Bright, most witty and most strenuous. There is a little of the satirist in Clara and she is keen enough to judge a compliment before it is uttered. But she is no cynic—her jolly disposition and sense of humor prevent that. One can neither imagine her flunking out nor burning the study lamp till morning.

name: Lawrence, Jacobson
motto: “At short-stop never idle; The dope-sheet was his favorite theme, The Baseball Guide, his Bible.”
description: Knows what a good time is and how to enjoy one. Has made other hits beside those on the baseball field—his ways are more than alluring. At every dance—goes only to hear the music! Thanks to him, Lovell and Geist enjoy increasing dividends. Of the kind that look compassionate when stinging with a practical joke.

name: Rheinhart, Kamplin
motto: “What shall I do to be forever known?”
description: A man with ideas of his own, a collector of much information which he disseminates freely. Always wears a busy, important air as if engaged in vast affairs of state. The very personification of helpfulness; he avoids that patronizing air peculiar to some men of genius. As obstinate as a mule, only more so.

name: James, Kennedy
motto: “I’m proud of all the Irish blood that’s in me; Divil a man can say a word agin me.”
description: A man of wide experience and varied travels, whose spiritual welfare would bear investigation and thorough study. His university career has been built somewhat in the order of a mosaic—piece by piece—and the result is a most marvelous and variegated pattern. A thoroughly good fellow who enjoys a quiet little time on state occasions.

name: Kishpaugh, Arthur W.
motto: "I'll live a private, pensive, single life."
description: One who watches the procession go by without participating in it. He believes that if you stand outside and watch you can see more. Exceedingly practical, he never wastes time in affairs of small moment. Almost every night he burns a candle or two at the shrine of knowledge, preparing for his career as an engineer. His future is ahead of him—which is not true of some of his worthy brothers.

name: Knudson, Inga
high school: Dazey, N. D. Valley City Normal, Washington University
motto: "Vivacity is a woman's unmeasured gift."
description: At first she debated the advisability of remaining with us, but luckily for us decided the matter in our favor. A musician who can make the violin say many things. She doesn't want to teach—we are not saying why. "Don't you know her? Why, she's the girl with the berry brown eyes and the Nell Brinkley hair."

name: Macdonald, Alec
motto: "I've taken my fun where I found it, I've rogued and I've roughed in my time."
description: A most strenuous fellow who, if the world please him not, proceeds to change the march of events. He fears nothing—not even Destiny. Fond of a good rough house and often engages in that gentle pastime. A doughty warrior upon the class football team—he has shown evidence of "red" blood.

name: McFadden, John Earl
motto: "For he had a natural talent at pleasing the sex."
description: McFadden: good looks, excellent fussing qualifications, omniscient, the only man to get advice from or to tell you how it was done. Always loaded with facts and will and does argue either side of any question. Believes that a man's best avocation is the amusement of the gentle sex. For the benefit of future generations he has left many literary productions—in the ink. Has many times averted a Postal Service deficit.

name: McIlraith, Annie
motto: "A little, pretty, witty, charming woman she."
description: "Annie hath a way"—bewitching, but not of the Salem kind. It is a way which wins the admiration of us all. So free is she from superficialities that just to meet her in the hall is to be persuaded anew of the reality of things worth while. She gives her time to many activities, but her business in life is to live.

name: Bertha, Mckechey
motto: "Is she not a heavenly saint? No, but she is an earthly paragon."
description: When you've been waiting in the hall for the maid for about forty minutes—to have the president of the Y.W. go and find the girl, makes you really feel grateful to the organization. Conscientious, reliable, a good student, she studies the Profs. and lessons equally. "I don't see why people call me good. I really am frivolous sometimes."

name: Howard, Maher
motto: "The warmth of genial courtesy, The calm of self-reliance."
description: Our social celebrity, you can always find his name in "among those present." Our Beau Brummel and Lord Chesterfield. Yea, verily, he should have lived in the days of chivalry—but we are glad that he is going through the world with us. An able man, well worthy of our regard.

name: C. Walter, Matscheck
motto: "'Tis strange how some men's temper suit To scowl, to argue and dispute."
description: An even tempered little chap who stands near the top of the class without making a fuss about it. Walks sedately through the world, oblivious of the petty things in life. So methodical in his speech that you can almost keep time by it. It is rumored that he was once seen to smile. (We refuse to vouch for the authenticity of that last statement, however.)

name: Matheson, Thomas Arthur
motto: “A man’s reach should exceed his grasp, Or what’s a heaven for.”
description: At the early age of six Tommy was discovered in the playroom with a beatific smile on his face and the sketch of a mule in his hand. By long practice he has brought the mule and his smile to such perfection that his smile is like that of a seraph and the mule like nothing in heaven or earth but itself. Added to being an artist, he is a most agreeable man on the gym floor.

name: May, Ethel J.
motto: “Your brain well furnished and your tongue well taught To press with energy your silent thought.”
description: Ethel is one of those clear, concise people whose every idea and fact is arranged and available. A possessor of many A’s, but her classes do not interfere with her university career—she has always aided the class in its task of managing the universe. “It’s just dreadful to have the reputation of being a shark. One can’t live it down if she try ever so hard.”

name: Murphy, Ella
motto: “I have not loved the world, but the world me. All I ask is to be let alone.”
description: A girl with independent ideas all her own. My Lady Disdain with a dislike for the genus homo which no flattery can overthrow nor loyalty conquer, and when you’ve known her you’ll agree that she’s positively unkind to the world in general and to some especially.

name: Nestoss, Anna
motto: "The secret of success is constancy of purpose."
description: As proof of this Anna is both successful and constant. In general it may be said that she lives along in her even mannered way, not deigning to mix in the light-headed joys of the majority of us. Her gain is our loss—here's hoping she "backslides" and joins with us in our attempt to eliminate care.

name: Opheim, Odd V.
motto: "But they, while their companions slept, Were toiling upward in the night."
description: There are some silent workers about the U. of N. D., but perhaps the most silent of all is Opheim. He delves day and night and when he has nothing else to dig into, he delves within himself. If you can make him come out of his shell you are a wonder and will find your deed to be its own reward.

name: Parizek, Rena L.
motto: "She is a most excellent lady."
description: And that's an admitted fact. There is no one in attendance at the University endowed with more patriotism and devotion to her class duties. There is an almost aggressive attitude incorporated in her general demeanor which makes her hard to overlook. Certainly no one does it.

name: Parizek, Wm. J.
motto: "That indolent but agreeable condition of doing nothing."
description: Bill is ever free from care and ready for a good time—a mighty good fellow who enjoys life and events as they happen to him. Has proposed strange and startling theories in Physics class; it's great to be ingenious. The Prof. thinks he has the right idea but can't express it.

name: Patterson, Orville D.
motto: "Have more than thou showest, Speak less than thou knowest."
description: A hard-working, conscientious little Irishman with a smile that's all his own. A fusser of the deepest dye, though you'd never suspect him of it. His sunny disposition is envied by many; in short, he is in every way a man of the first order. He should advertise but, being modest, doesn't.

motto: "I depend on my smile to make friends."

name: Quirke, Terrence T.
motto: "A fellow of infinite jest, of most excellent fancy."
description: His arrival is the most noteworthy fact since the coming of Columbus. With him he brought that quaint, curious and antiquated custom of studying, which he assures us is quite common over there. Were he to talk with President Taft, that dignitary would at the close of the interview go away with a feeling of having been honored. With Robert Louis Stevenson, Terry can say, "I know what pleasure is, for I have done good work."

name: Rex, Helen M.
motto: "A rosebud set with little wilful charms."
description: Pert, petite and pretty. A blithe little lady with a bewitching smile and a word of camaraderie for everyone. There is a twinkle in her eye which makes us wonder whether she is not wiser than she would have us know. What can we say? Woman has been the riddle of the ages; the modern college girl is the most puzzling enigma of all time.

Pert, petite and pretty. A blithe little lady with a bewitching smile and a word of camaraderie for everyone. There is a twinkle in her eye which makes us wonder whether she is not wiser than she would have us know. What can we say? Woman has been the riddle of the ages; the modern college girl is the most puzzling enigma of all time.

name: Schei, Hans A.
motto: "He brings a breath of the fiords."
description: Imperturbable and unassailable are the two adjectives expressive of Hans' dominant trait. He has no weaknesses of which his opponents may make advantages. He is also unafflicted with that great and almost universal drawback—a sense of humor.

name: Schmidt, Karl C.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: Karl holds the record in the high jump Marathon; for that reason the future author of a Treatise on Curves. Tennis is his pastime—'cause in that game you need a partner; he goes to classes only because it is customary. As an engineer Karl will undoubtedly put a few transition curves in the straight and narrow way; at least he's convinced that the line is out of date and needs less grade and a wider gauge.

name: Stoffer, Bernard M.
high school: St. Paul, Minn., Mechanics Arts High
motto: "Variety is the spice of life That gives it all its flavor."
description: And here we have much seasoning. A good worker; a seeker of the truth, a good judge of poor tobacco. Had an interesting mining trip—ask him about it! Has driven more instructors wild than any other man in the school. Upon closer scrutiny, we find method in his madness—he inquires to know, which is several shades better than letting things slide.

name: Turner, Genevieve
high school: Grand Forks, Berlin, Wis., High School
motto: "A kind and gentle heart she had To comfort friends and foes."
description: Miss Turner has come to us with a wide experience that serves as an adequate introduction and makes her unfailingly worthy of our esteem. She has had her part in making the world better and is therefore able to show us how the other half lives. No greater tribute can be given.

name: Harry, H. Van Ornum
motto: "Thy modesty's a flambeau to thy merit."
description: An easy-going son of toil who even loafs with nonchalance. The kind of fellow who comes in, stays a while and goes without raising any unnecessary disturbance. Crams before exams as a matter of habit—he could easily have gotten through without it. Fusses spasmodically—all of his late cases have been somewhat milder. Live and learn—you know.

name: Mildred, M. Veitch
motto: "It's guid to be merry and wise; It's guid to be honest and true."
description: Good-natured, jolly and lighthearted. Mildred is much interested in settlement work, and therein the world has much for which to be thankful. For a pretty girl with a soft voice and winsome smile can spread more civilization than a Carnegie library and the steel trust. As proof of her ability, her popularity—but why prove the obvious!

name: Theodore, B. Wells
motto: "The best of all Among the rarest of good ones."
description: Ted did great work as our pilot through a stormy Sophomore year and has saved our class basketball reputation many times. A track (cinder) athlete of several records; a thorough student. Wears a Mona Lisa smile which many of us wish were always labeled. It's terribly bothersome to look at a man and not be able to tell whether he's laughing at you or showing a smiling appreciation of your actions. Unassuming—but at social functions—he's there!

name: Watne, Carl A.
motto: "And still they gazed."
description: If you had ever heard Mr. Watne recite in History 10 you would know the meaning of the above quotation. He is a wonder when it comes to facts. He has not yet evolved a system of philosophy commensurate with his scheme of existence, but he may do that yet. A loyal Norseman who in all of his public speech causes the eagle to scream upon the subject of Scandinavian greatness.

name: Wiley, J. F. Deane
motto: "And he told a tale, He, with a wealth of reminiscence."
description: A great story teller is he—full of tales which would "hold children from their play and old men from the chimney corners". As a yell leader he makes more noise than a Dutch band. Deane is a merry fellow; one of those who can laugh with the world. In spite of all his faults we love him—still.

name: Zieske, George R.
motto: "Learn, sir, to jest in good time; There's a time for all things."
description: And in the yearly cycle George gets most of them done. An engineer with much breathless field experience. A Math. shark who can juggle integral signs most amazingly. He takes a helpless formula, differentiates, chases it from the numerator into the denominator, from plus to minus, to infinity and back again, through the Greek and Cuneiform alphabets, applies a slide rule and the method of least squares, integrates, and—presto change—we have the original again. Has an unholy stand-in with his Profs., but he doesn't know that the girls exist.

name: Dryden, John Raymond
high school: Grand Forks, Audubon, Iowa, High School
motto: "On their own merits modest men are dumb."
description: This shifty Varsity outfielder came to the University in the fall of '09. He is a bright, shining "Ray" as a baseball star, and they do say he is some "taters" at farming, for he farms and dissects Blackstone at the same time. We are all waiting for "Ray" to start some co-ed's heart palpitating.

name: Duffy, F. Clyde
motto: "I'll show you where the Circuit Court was wrong."
description: There is, however, one difference between Duffy and Kent, the other philosopher; Duffy hasn't been at it so long. In intercollegiate debates, the close-knit argument of his opponents has ever been as six-cent lace to his logical mind's eye. On the other hand, Duff can keep his mouth shut in three different languages, and it is never open except when he opens it.

name: Martin, William Flastad
motto: "The march of the human mind is slow."
description: This "individual" is one of the old timers. His patience is surpassed only by his unruffled calmness—be it known "he thinks in rhythm" and the equilibrium must not be thrown out of plumb. Martin has been known to take a girl to Hough's Dairy Lunch; most of the time he takes two. Mr. Flastad aspires to the governorship of Montana—his platform will be the Flastad Philosophy of Life.

name: Fletcher, Ernest Elbert
motto: "Good fellowship is the ship for me."
description: Lisbon claims the honor of being his birthplace. He has since become noted because he got his fifty cents back from the Men's Union. Bar examinations have no terrors for him, as he has a peculiar habit of coming to the Law School library for the purpose of studying. Although a hard worker, he has generally time to be sociable.

name: Harris, Wilbur Willing
high school: Morningside College of Sioux City, Iowa
motto: "He is so generally civil that no one thanks him for it."
description: Wilbur is another one of those who have strayed from their native State, the State of Iowa being his former domicile. He is a remarkably good student and has already shown himself worthy of the law class of '12. Mr. Harris came to the Law School in '10, after graduating from Morningside College, Iowa.

name: Jackman, William Lincoln
high school: Deering. English High School, Lynn, Mass.; State Normal, Bridgewater, Mass.
motto: "He is not at all a man who is not yet something of a boy."
description: Mr. Jackman came to the North Dakota University in 1910. He has studied law in Massachusetts but has decided to culminate his legal education in North Dakota. He is a typical Easterner, his New England brogue being his most distinguishing characteristic. William will make an honest and conscientious lawyer—a sort of Lincoln-Roosevelt man in one. He has already shown himself a shrewd cross-examiner.

name: Kime, Alvin M.
high school: New Rockford. Phillips Academy, Valparaiso University.
motto: "Wearing all that weight of learning lightly like a flower."
description: "Kime" first saw the light of day at Longmont, Colorado, but has since migrated to North Dakota. He was a member of the Philomathian debating team of '10 and proved himself an able and eloquent speaker. It has been rumored that he leads a "checkered" career at the Law School.

name: Mackoff, Herbert A.
high school: St. Paul. Townsend Harris High School, College of the City of New York.
motto: "And let him be sure to leave other men their turn to speak."
description: Mr. Mackoff's place of birth is in far-away Russia. He showed his good judgment when he migrated to North Dakota and selected the University Law School to aid him in learning the mysteries of law, in which he is already quite proficient. "Mac" is one of those who makes valuable use of his time, and has already distinguished himself in debating and oratory, being a member of the Philomathian debating team of 1910 and also representing that society in the oratorical contest of 1911.

name: Monson, Leigh Judd
motto: "Thou art a fellow of good respect."
description: Leigh entered the University in the fall of '10, and the fact that he had once attended Fargo College was found not to be against him. Mr. Monson commenced his study of law at Drake University, but he reconsidered and decided to finish the preparations for his chosen profession in the North Dakota University. In the class he has the happy faculty of asking all his questions at the same particular time and then causes no more annoyance for a week or two.

name: Murphy, Louis F.
motto: "Murphy is a grand old name."
description: Nature was generous in providing Murph with longitude, but his latitude is only mental. Yes, Louis Loo is broad minded and, withal, a democrat. He was born quite a while ago in Grand Forks, but hasn't lived here all his life; that is, not yet. He is our authority on Mortgages and buzz-wagons, which he claims are often chums. He has a clinging affection for Corporations.

name: Quigley, Walter E.
motto: "Some tell, some hear, some judge of news, some make it."
description: "Quig" decided after the Carleton-U. N. D. football game to enter the North Dakota Law School. He did so in 1909. Walter not only plays football, and runs long distances, but can wield the pen some. As yet he has not aroused the ire of the National Censorship Bureau. "Quig" believes in home products—where the heart is concerned.

name: Ray, C. Pinkham
motto: "This is my busy day."
description: In recognition of his ability to get things done, Pink has been made Business Manager of things in general around the University, and the term "things in general" includes those student organizations that seem to have any business—existing. He misses Moot Court only on special occasions, as, for instance, when he's with somebody or alone. Pink is a good student, a good mixer, and a man of decided convictions (not the police court kind, either).

name: Walter, J. Ray
motto: "How much will you give to boot?"
description: If Walt won't trade you something for your chances to the Presidency of the United States, then—you're a woman. But then, Walt comes from a horse country where "trades" are more numerous than professions and where they consider it better to pursue the legal profession than have the legal profession pursue you. No, the reason why our class president is at the University is not that the sheriff at home couldn't shoot straight. How dare you! He is a student and, above all, a diplomat.

name: George, F. Schafer
motto: "Persuasion tips his tongue whene'er he speaks."
description: Mr. Schafer is one of the most widely known students at the University. He takes an active part in debating and oratory, being a member of the intercollegiate debating teams during the years of 1909-1910-1911 and the winner of the first prize in the Stockwell oratorical contest of 1909. A better student it would be hard to find, conscientious, studious and exact.

name: Spaulding, Charles G.
motto: "Story! God bless you! I have none to tell you."
description: "Charlie" holds the Marathon for storytelling. He hasn't struck bottom yet, and he still tells rib-ticklers. Optimism takes up so much of his time that pessimism doesn't even come near him. "Charlie," as a general rule, is the woman in the case when Moot Court holds sway. His rosy blush would make any co-ed envious.

name: Trageton, O. O.
high school: Northwood. State Normal, Mayville, N. D.
motto: "He is the pineapple of politeness."
clubs: Member Executive Committee of Intercollegiate Civic League
description: Mr. Trageton is one of the progressive students of the University. He belongs to so many organizations that he finds it difficult to attend classes. He comes to us a graduate of Mayville Normal and has won distinction as a member of the victorious debating team that debated against the University of Manitoba. He is a genius in politics and certainly has not missed his calling.
